item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: As there's no indication the image was made of cc-compliant components, the image has been removed.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that a recent edit was reverted, as it was unnecessary. UK spelling variants of words are acceptable for SCP articles and do not need to be corrected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea is nice and interesting, but the way the main article is written is just… weird, and doesn't seem well done. If you could maybe rephrase most of it, then it would have my upvote. Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, and I uprooted it because I liked it. The only thing I don't like is that god-forsaken picture. Why did it have to be a close-up?

Seriously, that picture makes me queasy. Is there an alternative for it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: p l e a s e .

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the line: Our scans show that SCP-2059, the thing you are in, does not use more of itself. Why is that?

shouldn't that be MOST of itself rather than MORE of itself? or be phrased something like "Why does it not use more of itself"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do,dodododo dodo do du! dododo,dodododo dodo do du!

Nice one man. This is one of my favorite SCPs.

(not to mention how the name is the same name as the "Wall Of Flesh" boss from terraria.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do so like SCPs which have some semblance of a "happy ending". That is, as happy as a man trapped in a fleshy hell trying to help a child understand their existence can get.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: don't be rood ;c

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's actually a wall of flesh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well that's just honestly uncalled for. Faminepulse did a lot of work on it, it was made specifically for the scip.

Shame on you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Foundation took a picture and didn't let it redo its makeup first, of course it looks like crap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image for this SCP looks like crap

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was graciously provided for me by Faminepulse. Iirc, he combined a bunch of stuff together, including a squid eye and some gorey stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where's the pic from?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too bad it didn't take your fancy. Thanks for the read anyhow!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh. This is clearly going for some squick factor but there's nothing new or extreme enough for me to care.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually intended it to be a nod towards the creature's curiosity, but glad to know I am a genius without even knowing it myself. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Approx. 30 human beings
1 dead human being, in advanced stages of decomposition

This is where I upvoted. Its a small, but clever, way to show the horror of the situation indirectly. I had thought that everything absorbed into the creature died.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a picture, credit goes to Faminepulse. Ain't he a great fellow?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, I thought the "Garblord" and "Farshor" lines were a bit narmy, although I don't want to complain too hard about that because I actually think it's really neat to have the researchers trying to write things down. Also, some of the word choice was a bit off, as Kapitano largely covered.

Still, those are minor points. I liked this one a lot, and I especially liked how the first things on the list were a blue whale and a giant squid. Kind of how I imagine the thing getting started. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for squick and the Addenda. Although, I think we are getting to the point of having too many flesh blobs/body horror skips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heh, thanks for the compliments.

And yes, I know. But right now I only have 2 tales and 2 scips; in my books, that's not enough yet. When I make an author page, I want it to be presentable. We'll see after my next scip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You pulled it off, yay. Even if in the back of my mind there's still a (superficial) resemblance to SCP-171, this one's considerably more interesting, even while staying concise.

Well earned +1.

E: You're due to personnel page (and have been for while), BTW.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think what works about this is you've got a monster what incorporates people into itself, and a dude inside it who can talk to us, and he is just totally okay with this situation. And not even in a creepy "I am the chosen one and have become one with a higher power" way, he's just totally zen about everything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, it needs to be pretty clean in order to remain slippery, otherwise it would eventually climb out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a little confused by the containment procedures. Why is it so important that the chamber be glossy? I'm guessing it has something to do with this:

Spoiler
– hide block

SCP-2059-1: Why is there so little light? He cannot see anything.

…but I'm not really seeing how they got from that to "give it a really shiny cell".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmh, phrasing isn't one of my strengths. I'll give your suggestions a closer gander a bit later, but a few of them do sound better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, since it had clearly been in the ocean, I always figured some fishing vessel could've caught it in its net, and the Foundation finding out through the radio frequencies (if that would be the case, then prolly the crew is included in the mass).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An interesting take on the idea of a gestalt creature. A few oddities of phrasing seem jarring to me though.

+ Suggestions
- Hide

normally it follows a usual composition

Could you say "normally it adopts a default configuration", or something similar?

a grid of bones

Is it actually a lattice, or is it more of a scaffold?

not require extraneous sustenance

What does 'extraneous' mean here? If you mean 'sustenence is extraneous to it's needs' then you don't need to use the word at all.

Any creature touching the mass will be absorbed and "skinned"

Maybe something like "Any living creature touching SCP-2059 will be absorbed into its mass, but keratinous material (skin, hair and nails) is discarded."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought that it sounded like the Yellow Devil boss from Mega Man, myself. :)

Anyway, I enjoyed it quite a bit, and the present student/teacher connection between the monk and the object was unexpected and a great additional layer. I would love to know how the Foundation found out about this thing, but it's not a big deal. I also bet it smells terrible!

+1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eeeyup.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay, you posted it!

item.title: The light guides thee
item.content:encoded:
Well, here's my latest. Have fun.

((NOTE: This scip has no relation to the boss from Terraria. Do not question if it is that, I am sick of hearing of it.))

Note: I appreciate comments far more than votes, so please, speak your mind!
